metadata.name: Blitz — Riveteers Requisitioner casts with blitz, attacks, sacrificed at end step, two death triggers fire
metadata.description: P1 casts Riveteers Requisitioner for its Blitz cost {2}{R} (CR 702.152a: alternative cost). The permanent enters with haste. P1 attacks with the 3/1 — P2 has no blockers — dealing 3 combat damage to P2 (life 40 to 37). At the beginning of the end step the BlitzSacrificeTrigger fires and the creature is sacrificed (CR 702.152a). On death two triggers are placed on the stack in APNAP order (both controlled by P1, so stacked together then resolved LIFO per CR 405.5): (1) the Blitz draw-a-card trigger (CR 702.152a) and (2) the card's own static 'When this creature dies, create a Treasure token' trigger (CR 603.1). The Treasure token trigger resolves first (LIFO), creating a Treasure token on P1's battlefield. Then the draw trigger resolves, P1 draws a card. Final assertions verify: Riveteers Requisitioner in graveyard, Treasure token on P1 battlefield, P1 hand count +1 (from initial 0 to 1).
metadata.generation_notes: Riveteers Requisitioner (MCP verified + engine def at crates/engine/src/cards/defs/riveteers_requisitioner.rs): {1}{R}, Creature — Lizard Rogue, 3/1. Abilities: AbilityDefinition::Keyword(KeywordAbility::Blitz) + AbilityDefinition::Blitz { cost: ManaCost { generic: 2, red: 1 } } + AbilityDefinition::Triggered { trigger_condition: TriggerCondition::WhenDies, effect: Effect::CreateToken { spec: treasure_token_spec(1) } }. Blitz cost {2}{R}: generic 2 + red 1 = 3 total mana (more expensive than normal cost {1}{R}). CR 702.152a: when cast for the blitz cost, the permanent gains haste, gains the triggered ability 'When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, draw a card,' and a delayed trigger is created to sacrifice it at the beginning of the next end step. Harness action: 'cast_spell_blitz' (replay_harness.rs line 826) — finds the card in hand and sets cast_with_blitz: true. mana_paid: { generic: 2, red: 1 }. Turn 4 used so there is no ambiguity about summoning sickness; with haste (CR 702.10b) this is moot anyway. After entering the battlefield (power 3), P1 attacks — P2 controls no creatures so no blockers. Unblocked 3/1 assigns 3 damage to P2 (CR 510.1b): P2 life 40 to 37. End of combat and postcombat main priority rounds advance to end step. At beginning of end step: BlitzSacrificeTrigger fires; creature is sacrificed (CR 701.17a). On death (moved to graveyard), two triggers fire simultaneously (both controlled by P1, APNAP = P1 is active player): (1) the Blitz draw-a-card trigger added dynamically by the blitz cast (CR 702.152a) and (2) the static WhenDies Triggered ability for the Treasure token (CR 603.1). Per CR 603.3b (APNAP order for simultaneous triggers) and since both are P1's triggers, P1 places both on stack. P1 puts the Treasure trigger on first, then the draw trigger — so draw resolves last (wait: APNAP ordering for simultaneous triggers: each player puts their own in an order of their choice. P1 can choose order. LIFO: last-placed resolves first). Typical ordering: P1 places Treasure trigger, then draw trigger. Resolves LIFO: draw first, then Treasure. However, the script models P1 choosing Treasure first on stack and draw second — meaning draw resolves first (top of stack). Either ordering is correct per the rules; the key invariant is both resolve. Script uses: trigger_placed for sacrifice trigger, then sacrifice resolves, then two death triggers placed. P1's library must contain at least one card for draw to succeed. Treasure token spec: artifact token named 'Treasure' with '{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color.'

script.step_note: P1 casts Riveteers Requisitioner for its blitz cost {2}{R} (CR 702.152a: alternative cost). Both players pass priority; spell resolves, permanent enters battlefield with haste. Both players pass priority with empty stack to advance to beginning_of_combat.

actions.description: Initial: Riveteers Requisitioner in P1 hand; no permanents on battlefield; P2 at 40 life; stack empty; P1 has {2}{R} in mana pool
actions.note: Riveteers Requisitioner (3/1, Blitz {2}{R}) is in P1's hand. P1 has {2}{R} ready to pay the blitz cost. No permanents in play.

CR 702.152a: P1 casts Riveteers Requisitioner for its blitz cost {2}{R} (alternative cost, CR 601.2b). The permanent will gain haste, the draw-on-death trigger is conditionally active, and a delayed triggered ability is registered to sacrifice it at the beginning of the next end step. CR 601.2: Spell moves from hand to the stack.

actions.description: After blitz cast: Riveteers Requisitioner on stack (count 1); P1 hand empty
actions.note: CR 601.2: Riveteers Requisitioner is on the stack. The blitz cost {2}{R} has been paid. P1's hand is now empty.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Riveteers Requisitioner (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 702.152a: Riveteers Requisitioner enters the battlefield. Because the blitz cost was paid, it gains haste (cast_with_blitz = true) and the conditional draw-on-death trigger is active. A delayed triggered ability is registered: at the beginning of the next end step, sacrifice this permanent.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Creature spell resolves — permanent enters battlefield under P1's control with haste (CR 702.152a). BlitzSacrificeTrigger is queued for the beginning of the next end step.

actions.description: After resolution: Riveteers Requisitioner on P1 battlefield with haste; stack empty; life totals unchanged
actions.note: CR 702.152a: Riveteers Requisitioner (3/1) is on the battlefield with haste. Haste means it is not affected by summoning sickness (CR 702.10b) and may attack this turn.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance from precombat_main to beginning_of_combat step.

script.step_note: Beginning of combat step. No triggered abilities fire. Both players pass priority to advance to declare_attackers.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in beginning_of_combat with empty stack — advance to declare_attackers step.

script.step_note: P1 declares Riveteers Requisitioner as an attacker targeting P2. The 3/1 has haste so it may attack the turn it entered (CR 702.10b). It taps (CR 508.1f). Both players pass priority to advance to declare_blockers.

actions.note: CR 508.1: P1 declares Riveteers Requisitioner as an attacking creature targeting P2. CR 702.10b: Haste granted by blitz — summoning sickness does not apply. CR 508.1f: Creature taps as a side effect of attacking. CR 508.1k: Becomes an attacking creature.

actions.description: After declare_attackers: Riveteers Requisitioner tapped (attacking P2); stack empty
actions.note: CR 508.1f: Riveteers Requisitioner tapped as part of the attack declaration. It is now an attacking creature targeting P2.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to declare_blockers step.

script.step_note: P2 declares no blockers (P2 controls no creatures). Riveteers Requisitioner is unblocked (CR 509.1h). Both players pass priority — advance to combat_damage.

actions.note: CR 509.1: P2 declares no blockers — P2 controls no creatures. CR 509.1h: Riveteers Requisitioner has no blockers declared; it becomes an unblocked attacker.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority — advance to combat_damage step.

script.step_note: Riveteers Requisitioner (3/1, unblocked) assigns 3 combat damage to P2 (CR 510.1b). P2 life: 40 to 37. No triggered abilities from damage. Both players pass priority to advance to end_of_combat.

actions.note: CR 510.1: P1 assigns all 3 points of combat damage from Riveteers Requisitioner (power 3) to P2 — it is unblocked (CR 510.1b). CR 120.3: P2 loses 3 life.

actions.description: After combat damage: P2 at 37 life; Riveteers Requisitioner on P1 battlefield; stack empty
actions.note: CR 510.1b: Riveteers Requisitioner (power 3, unblocked) dealt 3 combat damage to P2. P2 life: 40 to 37. No triggered abilities from damage. Stack is empty.
CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority with empty stack — advance to end_of_combat step.

script.step_note: End of combat step. No triggered abilities fire. Both players pass priority to advance to postcombat_main.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in end_of_combat with empty stack — advance to postcombat_main step.

script.step_note: Postcombat main phase. Both players pass priority — advance to end step where the blitz sacrifice trigger will fire.

actions.note: CR 116.3d: Both players pass priority in postcombat_main — advance to end step (beginning of ending phase).

script.step_note: Beginning of end step: the BlitzSacrificeTrigger fires (CR 702.152a). It is placed on the stack, both players pass priority, and the trigger resolves: Riveteers Requisitioner is sacrificed. On death, two triggers fire simultaneously: (1) Blitz draw-a-card trigger (CR 702.152a) and (2) the static WhenDies Treasure token trigger (CR 603.1). P1 (active player) controls both; they are placed on the stack. Stack resolves LIFO: draw trigger resolves first (drawn card goes to P1 hand), then Treasure trigger resolves (Treasure token enters P1 battlefield).

actions.note: CR 702.152a: The delayed triggered ability created when Riveteers Requisitioner was cast with its blitz cost triggers at the beginning of the end step. CR 603.3: The BlitzSacrificeTrigger is placed on the stack. It will sacrifice the permanent when it resolves.

actions.description: After blitz sacrifice trigger fires: trigger on stack (count 1); Riveteers Requisitioner still on P1 battlefield
actions.note: The BlitzSacrificeTrigger is on the stack. Riveteers Requisitioner is still on the battlefield — it will only be sacrificed when the trigger resolves (CR 702.152a ruling: sacrificed only if still on battlefield when trigger resolves).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — the BlitzSacrificeTrigger (top of stack) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.17a: The BlitzSacrificeTrigger resolves — Riveteers Requisitioner is sacrificed. Its controller (P1) moves it from the battlefield to P1's graveyard. This is a 'dies' event (moved to graveyard from battlefield), which will trigger both the blitz draw-a-card condition and the static WhenDies Treasure token ability.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Trigger resolves. CR 701.17a: Riveteers Requisitioner is sacrificed — moved to P1's graveyard. Its death simultaneously triggers: (1) the blitz draw-a-card trigger (CR 702.152a) and (2) the WhenDies Treasure token trigger (CR 603.1).

actions.description: After sacrifice: Riveteers Requisitioner in P1 graveyard; NOT on P1 battlefield; two death triggers on stack
actions.note: Riveteers Requisitioner is in P1's graveyard. Both death triggers (blitz draw and WhenDies Treasure) have been placed on the stack (count 2). Stack resolves LIFO (CR 405.5).
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — the top trigger on the stack (blitz draw-a-card trigger) resolves first (LIFO, CR 405.5).

resolution.note: CR 702.152a: 'When this permanent is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, draw a card.' P1 draws one card from their library. P1 hand count: 0 to 1.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Blitz draw trigger resolves. P1 draws a card (top card of library: Lightning Bolt). P1 hand count increases from 0 to 1.

actions.description: After blitz draw trigger resolves: P1 hand count is 1; Treasure trigger still on stack (count 1)
actions.note: P1 has drawn one card from the blitz draw trigger. The Treasure token trigger is still on the stack. Life totals unchanged.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — the remaining trigger (WhenDies Treasure token) resolves.

resolution.note: CR 603.1 + 111.1: 'When this creature dies, create a Treasure token.' A Treasure artifact token is created on P1's battlefield under P1's control. CR 111.5: Tokens exist only on the battlefield.

actions.note: CR 608.2: WhenDies Treasure token trigger resolves. A Treasure token enters P1's battlefield (CR 111.1, 111.5). The Treasure token is an artifact with '{T}, Sacrifice this token: Add one mana of any color.'

actions.description: Final: Riveteers Requisitioner in P1 graveyard; Treasure token on P1 battlefield; P1 hand count 1; P2 at 37 life; stack empty
actions.note: CR 702.152a blitz scenario complete. Riveteers Requisitioner: cast with blitz {2}{R}, entered with haste, attacked for 3 damage (P2: 40 to 37), sacrificed at end step by blitz trigger. Two death triggers fired and resolved: blitz draw (P1 drew one card) and WhenDies Treasure (Treasure token on P1 battlefield). Stack is empty.
